items.title: [The Verge] Indie Pass is a new subscription service just for indie games
items.content_html: Indie.io, which offers a platform to help independent game developers publish their titles, is launching a new subscription service called Indie Pass. The service will cost $6.99 per month and have more than 70 titles, including indie.io's Echoes of the Plum Grove and games from the Fire Emblem-like Dark Deity series. Indie Pass launches on […]

items.title: [The Verge] Transfer Point is a modern adventure game made with 40-year-old software
items.content_html: One of the year's most intriguing games was developed using software first released 40 years ago. Transfer Point looks and plays like a classic Mac point-and-click adventure game, and there's a very good reason for that: It was developed using World Builder, a game creation tool first released in 1986 that has since become freeware. […]

items.title: [Open Culture] How James Cameron Shot Titanic/i>‘s Hugely Complex Sinking Scene

But according to reasonable reckonings, James Cameron may have directed not just one but several of the most expensive movies of all time. The underwater sci-fi spectacle that was The Abyss necessitated one of the biggest production budgets of the […]

items.title: [Boing Boing] A Pulitzer winner wrote a short story without using the letter "I"
items.content_html:
In 1969, French author Georges Perec wrote La Disparition, an entire novel that does not contain the letter E. In 1994, Gilbert Adair translated it into English as A Void — also without a single E. I've only read portions of it, and I'm still amazed that someone could tell a coherent story without the most common letter in the English language. — Read the rest

The post A Pulitzer winner wrote a short story without using the letter "I" appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Artist paints Lovecraft's Great Old Ones into all 46 of Hokusai's woodblock prints
items.content_html:
Katsushika Hokusai drew tentacled sea creatures in the 1800s. H.P. Lovecraft built a mythology around them in the 1920s. Japanese illustrator Goki Yamada has now put them together in Thirty-six Views of Evil Gods (邪神三十六景), a 128-page art book that inserts Cthulhu, Nyarlathotep, and other Great Old Ones into every plate of Hokusai's famous ukiyo-e series "Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji" — the woodblock prints that gave us "The Great Wave off Kanagawa." — Read the rest

The post Artist paints Lovecraft's Great Old Ones into all 46 of Hokusai's woodblock prints appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [Boing Boing] This Twilight ripoff is so bad it became a cult sensation

Nicholson is very much a pillar of nerd culture on the internet — the kind of nerd who doesn't go around calling people slurs on Twitter. — Read the rest
